content.rendered:
Larry Williams released “Slow Down” in 1958, and its influence exceeded its initial popularity.

“Slow Down” was considered to be an R&B single, but it hit the radar and captured the imagination of emerging rock artists like the Young Rascals, and of course The Beatles.

Bruce has never covered “Slow Down” in concert, but he did perform it once, in the unlikeliest of venues: at the now-defunct Classics Cafe in Westwood, New Jersey in 1994. What brought Bruce to the Cafe is unbeknownst to me, but once there he took the opportunity to join cover band Stand and Deliver for six songs, including Bruce’s one-and-only performance of “Slow Down.” It’s loose, fun, and although Bruce doesn’t contribute vocals, he sure seems to be having fun on guitar.

Slow Down
First performed: August 26, 1994 (Westwood, NJ)
Last performed: August 26, 1994 (Westwood, NJ)

© January 13, 2019

excerpt.rendered: One time only: Bruce covers Larry Williams’ influential “Slow Down” with a cover band at the Classics Cafe in Westwood, New Jersey.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Hippy Hippy Shake
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: From the Legends of Springsteen files: Bruce and Max join cover band Stand and Deliver at the Classics Cafe in Westwood for rocking rendition of "Hippy Hippy Shake."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Everybody’s Trying to Be My Baby
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joins Steve Earle on stage for a cover of a rock classic that goes much further back than you might think.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: Slow Fade
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Still missing in action after all these years: whatever happened to the smoking E Street Band song known only as "Slow Fade" from bootleg rehearsal tapes? Take a listen to this remarkable track inside.

jetpack-related-posts.title: I Saw Her Standing There
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Rock royalty gathers in 1988 to induct The Beatles into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame, and Bruce takes a turn at the mic on their 1963 classic.

jetpack-related-posts.title: MatR: Bruce Springsteen and Dr. John: Something You Got
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: When Bruce and Dr. John performed together for the first time, they reached way back in both of their set lists to find the perfect song.
